it:80.2: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
it:80.3:
"There are these three kinds of unskillful thinking. Which three? Thinking concerned with not wanting to be despised; thinking concerned with gains, offerings, and tribute; thinking concerned with an empathy for others.
There are three kinds of unskillful thinking."
it:80.4: Fettered to not wanting to be despised; to gains, offerings, respect; to delight in companions: you're far from the ending of fetters.
it:80.5: But whoever here, having abandoned sons, cattle, marriage, intimates: he's capable — a monk like this — of touching superlative self-awakening.
it:80.6: This, too, was the meaning of what was said by the Blessed One, so I have heard.
